coach_phrase: Brex is great if you're a US-based startup with USD-only spend

lines.speaker: L.D.
lines.text: I saw you closed your Series-B last quarter — how are you handling per-employee card issuance for the new hires?

lines.speaker: L.D.
lines.text: Brex is great if you're a US-based startup with USD-only spend. We're built for EU multi-currency and we sit on top of your existing accounts — additive, not a replacement.

lines.speaker: L.D.
lines.text: What does your FX exposure look like on this quarter's spend?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Honestly — I don't know off the top of my head.

lines.speaker: L.D.
lines.text: 90% of founders don't. That's the wedge — want a 10-minute look at last month's FX cost vs Brex?

coach_phrase: so you've got the card-issuing side sorted

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: We already have a Visa from our bank.

lines.speaker: M.G.
lines.text: So you've got the card-issuing side sorted.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Yeah — but the controls are a mess. We had a $1,200 charge nobody could explain for 2 weeks last quarter.

lines.speaker: M.G.
lines.text: Got it — visibility is the broken thing, not the card. What does your bookkeeper do when a charge shows up that nobody recognises?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: She emails everyone and waits. Sometimes it takes two weeks.

lines.speaker: buyer
lines.text: We already have a corporate Visa from our bank.

lines.speaker: H.K.
lines.text: But our fees are lower — and the FX is better.

lines.speaker: buyer
lines.text: I don't think you understand. We're fine.

lines.speaker: P.B.
lines.text: I know you're busy, but our card is cheaper than your bank's.

lines.speaker: buyer
lines.text: Cheaper than what? I didn't say I had a problem.
